run.final_message:
I found a few datasets that might provide insights into educational outcomes in Canada:

1. [Wages by education level](/datasets/1f14addd-e4fc-4a07-9982-ad98db07ef86): This dataset provides median hourly wages for different education levels, age groups, and types of work across various geographic regions in Canada and Ontario, spanning from 2006 to 2024. While it focuses on wages, it can indirectly reflect the value of education over time.

2. [Indices of deprivation](/datasets/004de02c-19f1-4da0-9af8-33f893e41972): This dataset includes indices of deprivation for educational institutions in Quebec, such as the Index of Material and Social Exclusion and the School Failure Rate, covering the academic year 2025-2026. It provides insights into educational challenges and outcomes in specific regions.

3. [Performance results of the Exchanges Canada sub-program](/datasets/929013e9-75b2-4e43-8b88-634062b91b59): This dataset focuses on participant feedback regarding their understanding of Canadian shared experiences, with data from 2016-2017 to 2018-2019. It provides a specific view of educational program outcomes.

These datasets offer different perspectives on education, from economic outcomes to institutional challenges. If you are interested in a specific aspect, such as graduation rates or standardized test scores, please let me know, and I can refine the search further.
